paragraphs.en: You -- no, no, you're going to -- you're going to ignore the moral conversation around Ken Paxton?
paragraphs.cn: Anda -- -- tidak, tidak, Anda akan -- -- Anda akan mengabaikan diskusi moral seputar Ken Paxton?

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: No, no. No, here's the moral --
paragraphs.cn: Tidak, tidak. Tidak, ini moral -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: You're going to ignore the moral conversation around Max Miller beating his wife in front of his kids.
paragraphs.cn: Anda akan mengabaikan diskusi moral seputar Max Miller yang memukuli istrinya di depan anak-anak?

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Here's the moral conversation. Here's the -- here's the moral conversation in a democracy.
paragraphs.cn: Ini diskusi moral. Ini -- -- inilah diskusi moral dalam sistem demokrasi.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: You can't -- you can't come to us and say that these things --
paragraphs.cn: Anda tidak bisa -- -- Anda tidak bisa datang kepada kami dan mengatakan hal-hal ini -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: In a democracy voters get to decide.
paragraphs.cn: Dalam demokrasi, pemilih berhak memutuskan.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: Somehow or another we should ignore those things because of somehow or another some right-wing policy issues --
paragraphs.cn: Entah bagaimana, kita harus mengabaikan hal-hal ini karena masalah kebijakan sayap kanan tertentu -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: In a democracy, voters get to choose their party leader (ph).
paragraphs.cn: Dalam demokrasi, pemilih berhak memilih pemimpin partai mereka.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right, I'm going to bring it back to a female victim. I want to just repeat this before we go. This is Lyndsey Fifield, who is the accuser against Graham Platner, talking about violence and abuse. And she says something that I think we should remember going forward as we're trying to decide politically, as a political class, what matters in a man's history.
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, saya akan mengembalikan topik ke seorang korban wanita. Sebelum kita selesai, saya ingin mengulangi kutipan ini. Ini Lindsey Field, penuduh Graham Platter, berbicara tentang kekerasan dan pelecehan. Dia mengatakan sesuatu yang saya pikir harus kita ingat saat kita bergerak maju, karena kita sebagai kelas politik sedang mencoba memutuskan secara politik apa yang penting dalam sejarah seorang pria.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And she says, "the things that he does in private, in his relationships with women, the things that he has done to women, coercive, control, lying, deception, abuse, and really horrible things that we know about him," she says, "we know that you cannot be that way in private and not have it translate into how you would govern." You guys, stay with us. We're going to come back. Right now I want to show you live images from New York because they are actually trying to stabilize a high rise.
paragraphs.cn: Dia berkata: 'Apa yang dia lakukan secara pribadi, dalam hubungannya dengan wanita, apa yang dia lakukan pada wanita -- -- paksaan, kontrol, kebohongan, penipuan, pelecehan, dan hal-hal mengerikan yang kita ketahui tentang dia,' katanya, 'Kita tahu, Anda adalah orang seperti itu secara pribadi, tidak mungkin itu tidak terbawa ke cara Anda memerintah.' Hadirin, tetaplah bersama kami. Kami akan segera kembali. Sekarang saya ingin menunjukkan kepada Anda gambar langsung dari New York karena mereka sedang mencoba menstabilkan gedung tinggi.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Crews are working overnight to shore up the weakest parts of a building.
paragraphs.cn: Para pekerja bekerja semalaman untuk memperkuat bagian terlemah dari bangunan.

paragraphs.speaker: Scott Jennings
paragraphs.en: We talk for about 17 minutes. You know, he talked a lot about the situation with Iran. He talked a little bit about Ukraine and what's going on in Europe. I'd gotten a few text messages from him, but I hadn't spoken to him. So, it was good to hear his voice. His voice sounded strong and sounded like he was keeping up with the news to me.
paragraphs.cn: Kami berbicara sekitar 17 menit. Anda tahu, dia banyak berbicara tentang situasi di Iran. Dia sedikit berbicara tentang Ukraina dan Eropa. Saya sebelumnya menerima beberapa SMS darinya, tetapi tidak berbicara dengannya. Jadi, senang mendengar suaranya. Suaranya terdengar kuat, dan saya pikir dia terus mengikuti berita.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, Senator Mitch McConnell has been hospitalized for three weeks. Still unclear why or when he'll be released. And yet some MAGA voices demanding, quote, "proof of life." Here's an example. Laura Loomer posting, "high level source close to the White House tells me, quote, 'Mitch McConnell is officially brain dead and he's not coming back'." Again, that is Laura Loomer on X saying that. And then this is a former senior adviser for Kamala Harris' campaign, Mike Nellis.
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, Senator Mitch McConnell telah dirawat di rumah sakit selama tiga minggu. Masih belum jelas mengapa dia dirawat atau kapan akan keluar. Namun, beberapa suara MAGA menuntut apa yang disebut 'bukti hidup'. Ini contohnya. Laura Loomer memposting: 'Sumber tingkat tinggi yang dekat dengan Gedung Putih mengatakan kepada saya, Mitch McConnell secara resmi mati otak, dia tidak akan kembali.' Sekali lagi, ini dari Laura Loomer di X. Kemudian Mike Nellis, mantan penasihat senior kampanye Kamala Harris.

paragraphs.speaker: Mike Nellis
paragraphs.en: If Scott Jennings and Thune and everybody else could have a 20 minute phone call with Mitch McConnell, he should be able to shoot a 60 second proof of life video to show us that he's OK. I think they know the deadline for a special election is August 3rd. If he's dead or if he's on a ventilator, if he's not able to do his job, he should resign right now. They should have a special election.
paragraphs.cn: Jika Scott Jennings, Thune, dan yang lainnya bisa menelepon Mitch McConnell selama 20 menit, dia seharusnya bisa merekam video bukti hidup 60 detik yang menunjukkan bahwa dia baik-baik saja. Saya pikir mereka tahu batas waktu pemilihan khusus adalah 3 Agustus. Jika dia sudah mati, atau hidup dengan ventilator, tidak dapat menjalankan tugasnya, dia harus mengundurkan diri sekarang. Mereka harus mengadakan pemilihan khusus.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, you have a lot of Senate Republicans coming out. We're going to just show some on screen, Isaac, all saying, I talked to him, I talked to him, I talked to him for 20 minutes, I talked to him for 45 minutes. We heard -- we're hearing these pundits talk not just about his potential health, but what they think the issue is, a special election deadline.
paragraphs.cn: Jadi, banyak senator Republik yang tampil. Kami akan menampilkan beberapa di layar, Isaac, semuanya berkata: Saya berbicara dengannya, saya berbicara dengannya, saya berbicara 20 menit dengannya, saya berbicara 45 menit dengannya. Kami mendengar -- -- kami mendengar para ahli ini tidak hanya berbicara tentang potensi masalah kesehatannya, tetapi juga apa yang mereka pikir menjadi masalah: batas waktu pemilihan khusus.

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.en: Yes. Look, Audie, if you were -- hadn't been on air for three weeks and there was no answers where you had gone, probably CNN would be interested to know what was going on with you.
paragraphs.cn: Ya. Dengar, Audi, jika Anda tidak muncul di acara selama tiga minggu dan tidak ada yang tahu ke mana Anda pergi, mungkin CNN akan ingin tahu apa yang terjadi pada Anda.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, although Reddit would say thank you.
paragraphs.cn: Ya, meskipun Reddit akan berterima kasih.

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.en: Same thing with -- sure. Maybe.
paragraphs.cn: Hal yang sama -- -- tentu saja. Mungkin.

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.en: I think the same would be true of me probably for you guys, too.
paragraphs.cn: Saya pikir bagi Anda, saya mungkin juga sama.

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.en: Mitch McConnell is entitled to private information about his health, but he is a public official. He is the taxpayers of Kentucky and of the federal government -- and of the country, because he's a federal employee, are entitled to know what's going on.
paragraphs.cn: Mitch McConnell berhak merahasiakan informasi kesehatannya, tetapi dia adalah pejabat publik. Dia adalah pembayar pajak Kentucky dan federal -- -- juga pembayar pajak negara, karena dia adalah pegawai federal, berhak tahu apa yang terjadi.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: Isaac, this is the second Republican who we've had these medical mishaps, if you will, missing in action. And I just think that from the party who claims transparency and accountability, we should just know more when these situations pop up. We had a member of Congress who was missing in action for months.
paragraphs.cn: Isaac, ini adalah orang Republik kedua yang mengalami keadaan darurat medis seperti ini, bisa dibilang hilang. Saya hanya berpikir bahwa untuk partai yang mengklaim transparansi dan akuntabilitas, ketika hal seperti ini terjadi, kita harus tahu lebih banyak. Kami memiliki seorang anggota Kongres yang hilang selama berbulan-bulan.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: And we didn't hear anything. And we later learned depression. Now we have Senator McConnell. If this were Democrats, Republicans would be wetting their diapers about accountability and responsibility.
paragraphs.cn: Kami tidak mendengar apa-apa. Kemudian kami tahu itu depresi. Sekarang ada Senator McConnell. Jika ini adalah Demokrat, Partai Republik pasti sudah kebelet pipis karena tuntutan akuntabilitas dan tanggung jawab.

paragraphs.speaker: Dovere
paragraphs.en: If these are -- if these are important jobs, as they are --
paragraphs.cn: Jika ini adalah pekerjaan penting, sebagaimana memang -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Well, it's just interesting because I am old enough to remember the Biden years where we had our secretary of defense absolutely AWOL, went out for a surgery for, I think, three weeks. We had the president of the United States obviously suffering from some type of a mental affliction and suffering, dementia (ph).
paragraphs.cn: Yah, ini menarik, karena saya cukup tua untuk mengingat masa pemerintahan Biden, ketika Menteri Pertahanan kami benar-benar meninggalkan tugasnya untuk menjalani operasi, saya pikir tiga minggu. Presiden AS kami jelas menderita penyakit mental dan penderitaan, demensia.

paragraphs.speaker: Seawright
paragraphs.en: OK, do you have medical proof of that?
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, apakah Anda memiliki bukti medis?

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Well, I saw it on TV. I saw it would my own eyes. (CROSS TALK)
paragraphs.cn: Yah, saya melihatnya di TV. Saya melihatnya dengan mata kepala sendiri. (Percakapan silang)

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Well, hold on. Let me get -- let me bring you one other thing.
paragraphs.cn: Baiklah, tunggu. Biarkan saya -- -- biarkan saya menyebut satu hal lagi.

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: But we were gaslit and told that he was at the top of his game.
paragraphs.cn: Tapi kami dibohongi, diberi tahu bahwa dia dalam kondisi terbaik.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We're going to give the last word to Lloyd Austin, who you just mentioned.
paragraphs.cn: Akhirnya kami memberikan kesempatan bicara kepada Lloyd Austin, yang baru saja Anda sebutkan.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Because back in 2024, he answered questions about this. And this is a great way to end the show. Here is the lesson he took away from being private about your health and not telling the public.
paragraphs.cn: Karena pada tahun 2024, dia menjawab tentang masalah ini. Ini cara yang bagus untuk mengakhiri acara. Berikut adalah pelajaran yang dia petik dari merahasiakan kesehatannya sendiri dan tidak memberi tahu publik tentang hal ini.

paragraphs.speaker: Lloyd Austin
paragraphs.en: I don't think it's news that I'm a pretty private guy. I never liked burdening others with my problems. It's just not my way. But I've learned from this experience. Taking this kind of job means losing some of the privacy that most of us expect. The American people have a right to know if their leaders are facing health challenges that might affect their ability to perform their duties, even temporarily.
paragraphs.cn: Saya menganggap diri saya orang yang cukup menjaga privasi, ini bukan berita baru. Saya tidak suka merepotkan orang lain dengan masalah saya sendiri. Itu bukan gaya saya. Tapi pengalaman ini mengajarkan saya banyak hal. Memegang jabatan seperti ini berarti melepaskan privasi yang kebanyakan dari kita harapkan. Rakyat Amerika berhak tahu apakah pemimpin mereka menghadapi tantangan kesehatan yang dapat mempengaruhi kemampuan mereka untuk menjalankan tugas, bahkan jika sementara.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And later on, testified before Congress that there would be changes made to notify the chain of command, as well as the public. There is a way for Congress and lawmakers to deal with this. They have not chosen to. I want to thank you guys for being here. I want to thank you for waking up with me. I'm Audie Cornish, and the headlines are next. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: Kemudian, saya bersaksi di Kongres bahwa akan ada perubahan, memberi tahu rantai komando dan publik. Kongres dan legislator punya cara untuk menangani masalah ini. Tapi mereka memilih untuk tidak melakukannya. Terima kasih sudah datang ke sini. Terima kasih sudah bangun bersama saya. Saya Audie Cornish, berikutnya adalah berita utama.

vocabulary.en: a non-binding agreement outlining the terms and details of an understanding between parties
vocabulary.excerpt: President Trump says the memorandum of understanding with Iran is over.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Presiden Trump mengatakan nota kesepahaman dengan Iran telah berakhir.

vocabulary.en: to impose again, especially a restriction or penalty
vocabulary.excerpt: reimposed sanctions on Iranian oil in retaliation for attacks on ships near the Strait of Hormuz.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Memberlakukan kembali sanksi minyak Iran sebagai pembalasan atas serangan terhadap kapal di dekat Selat Hormuz.

vocabulary.en: a response that is overwhelmingly destructive or decisive
vocabulary.excerpt: Iran said it would deliver a, quote, 'crushing response.'
vocabulary.exampleCn: 伊朗表示将发出'毁灭性回应'。

vocabulary.en: a contemptible or worthless person or group
vocabulary.excerpt: They're scum. You know what scum is? They're scum.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他们是败类。你知道败类是什么吗？他们是败类。

vocabulary.en: crazy or foolish
vocabulary.excerpt: He called them scum. He said that they were cuckoo
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他称他们为败类，说他们疯了。

vocabulary.en: military attacks carried out in response to an attack
vocabulary.excerpt: We did see last night the president ordering up those retaliatory strikes on Iran.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我们昨晚确实看到总统下令对伊朗进行报复性打击。

vocabulary.en: official permissions to bypass sanctions
vocabulary.excerpt: He also pulled back those sanctions waivers that he had put on the sales of Iranian oil.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他还撤回了对伊朗石油销售的制裁豁免。

vocabulary.en: a small sign of hope
vocabulary.excerpt: Although the president does say that there's a glimmer of hope that these negotiations can continue.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Meskipun presiden memang mengatakan bahwa negosiasi masih memiliki secercah harapan untuk dilanjutkan.

vocabulary.en: intended to gain goodwill or to reduce hostility
vocabulary.excerpt: perhaps a more conciliatory president would come here
vocabulary.exampleCn: Mungkin seorang presiden yang lebih bersedia untuk bersikap rekonsiliatif akan datang ke sini.

vocabulary.en: feelings of resentment over something believed to be unfair
vocabulary.excerpt: all of his grievances and all of his frustrations at NATO have not waned one bit.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Semua ketidakpuasan dan frustrasinya terhadap NATO tidak berkurang sedikit pun.

vocabulary.en: to an extent that is too large or too small in comparison with something else
vocabulary.excerpt: We pay disproportionately.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Kami membayar secara tidak proporsional tinggi.

vocabulary.en: to increase something in a series of steps
vocabulary.excerpt: I think President Trump is just trying to ratchet up the visible frustration
vocabulary.exampleCn: Saya pikir Presiden Trump hanya ingin secara bertahap meningkatkan frustrasi yang terlihat.

vocabulary.en: a system or method of exercising authority or domination
vocabulary.excerpt: where they can gradually establish a regime of control.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Di sana mereka secara bertahap dapat membangun sistem kontrol.

vocabulary.en: permission to proceed with an action
vocabulary.excerpt: Does that mean Israel still has a kind of green light to continue its fight with Hezbollah in Lebanon?
vocabulary.exampleCn: Apakah ini berarti Israel masih memiliki lampu hijau untuk terus berperang melawan Hizbullah Lebanon?

vocabulary.en: an extremely difficult or complex problem
vocabulary.excerpt: And it is at the center of this kind of Gordian knot of international issues
vocabulary.exampleCn: Ini berada di pusat masalah rumit dalam isu internasional ini.

vocabulary.en: a temporary suspension of fighting
vocabulary.excerpt: If the ceasefire is over, Does that mean Israel still has a kind of green light
vocabulary.exampleCn: Jika gencatan senjata berakhir, apakah ini berarti Israel masih memiliki semacam lampu hijau?

vocabulary.en: a set of strategies or tactics used by someone
vocabulary.excerpt: Yes, it is. No, that is the playbook here.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Ya, itulah praktik umumnya.

vocabulary.en: to admit that something is true or that you have been defeated
vocabulary.excerpt: He will do that, and he'll never concede anything.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他会那样做，而且从不做出任何让步。

vocabulary.en: available for discussion or consideration
vocabulary.excerpt: he says, yes, of course, it's still on the table.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他说，是的，当然，它仍然摆在桌面上。

vocabulary.en: to accept or tolerate something unpleasant
vocabulary.excerpt: Try to get to something that he can actually live with
vocabulary.exampleCn: 试图达成他实际上能够接受的结果。

vocabulary.en: in a position of control or authority
vocabulary.excerpt: I think President Trump would like to feel that he's in the driver's seat.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我认为特朗普总统希望感觉自己处于主导地位。

vocabulary.en: to pursue or attack someone
vocabulary.excerpt: We feel free to go after them again, now.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Kami sekarang bebas untuk menyerang mereka lagi.

vocabulary.en: a meeting of the heads of state of NATO member countries
vocabulary.excerpt: Details out of the NATO summit.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Detail KTT NATO.

vocabulary.en: a narrow strait between the Persian Gulf and the Gulf of Oman, a strategic oil shipping route
vocabulary.excerpt: attacks on ships near the Strait of Hormuz.
vocabulary.exampleCn: Serangan terhadap kapal di dekat Selat Hormuz.

sentences.en: The comments this morning from President Trump come after the U.S. military hit more than 80 Iranian targets on Tuesday and, in its biggest move yet, reimposed sanctions on Iranian oil in retaliation for attacks on ships near the Strait of Hormuz.
sentences.cn: Pernyataan Presiden Trump pagi ini disampaikan setelah militer AS pada hari Selasa menyerang lebih dari 80 target Iran dan mengambil langkah terbesarnya sejauh ini, yaitu memberlakukan kembali sanksi minyak terhadap Iran sebagai balasan atas serangan terhadap kapal di dekat Selat Hormuz.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama (The comments...come after...) + klausa waktu (after the U.S. military hit...and...reimposed...), di mana and menghubungkan dua predikat paralel, 'in its biggest move yet' sebagai sisipan, 'in retaliation for...' sebagai frasa preposisi yang berfungsi sebagai keterangan tujuan.
sentences.analysis: 1. 'come after' menunjukkan urutan waktu; 2. 'in its biggest move yet' dengan 'yet' digunakan setelah superlatif untuk memperkuat; 3. 'in retaliation for' adalah ekspresi umum dalam berita, berarti 'sebagai balasan atas'; 4. Kalimat memadatkan banyak informasi melalui klausa waktu dan sisipan.

sentences.en: Iran said it would deliver a, quote, 'crushing response.'
sentences.cn: Iran mengatakan akan memberikan, mengutip kata-katanya, 'tanggapan yang menghancurkan'.
sentences.structure: Klausa utama (Iran said) + klausa objek (it would deliver...), dengan 'quote' sebagai sisipan yang memperkenalkan kutipan langsung.
sentences.analysis: 1. 'quote' dalam berita digunakan untuk memperkenalkan kutipan langsung, berarti 'mengutip kata-katanya'; 2. 'crushing response' adalah frasa umum dalam berita, berarti 'tanggapan yang menghancurkan'; 3. Meskipun pendek, kalimat mengandung struktur sisipan kutipan langsung.

sentences.en: But as far as Trump is concerned, he is done negotiating with Iran.
sentences.cn: Namun, bagi Trump, dia sudah selesai bernegosiasi dengan Iran.
sentences.structure: 'as far as...is concerned' sebagai frasa tetap yang berfungsi sebagai keterangan, klausa utama adalah 'he is done negotiating with Iran'.
sentences.analysis: 1. 'as far as...is concerned' berarti 'sejauh menyangkut...'; 2. 'be done doing sth.' adalah ekspresi informal, berarti 'selesai melakukan sesuatu' atau 'tidak lagi melakukan sesuatu'; 3. Kalimat ini adalah kalimat pernyataan yang umum dalam berita.

sentences.en: And they're vicious, violent people, and if they had a nuclear weapon, they'd use it.
sentences.cn: Mereka adalah orang-orang yang kejam dan kasar, dan jika mereka memiliki senjata nuklir, mereka akan menggunakannya.
sentences.structure: Kalimat majemuk (And they're...people, and...), klausa kedua mengandung klausa pengandaian dengan if (if they had..., they'd use...).
sentences.analysis: 1. 'vicious, violent' adalah kata sifat paralel yang menerangkan 'people'; 2. Pengandaian menunjukkan asumsi yang berlawanan dengan kenyataan saat ini; 3. Kalimat ini mencerminkan nada tegas Trump.

sentences.en: But what that actually means for the negotiations and for the war are a little bit unclear.
sentences.cn: Namun, apa artinya ini sebenarnya bagi negosiasi dan perang masih belum jelas.
sentences.structure: 主语从句（what that actually means...）作主语，谓语为are a little bit unclear。
sentences.analysis: 1. 主语从句what...means作主语，谓语用单数或复数均可，此处用are可能为口语化；2. 'a little bit'是程度副词；3. 新闻中常用此类表达不确定性。

sentences.en: Most accounts suggest that those talks had been very, very slow going, even though they were up against this 60-day deadline.
sentences.cn: 大多数报道表明，这些谈判进展非常非常缓慢，尽管它们面临60天的最后期限。
sentences.structure: 主句（Most accounts suggest）+ 宾语从句（that those talks had been...），从句中包含even though引导的让步状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 1. 'accounts'在此意为'报道、说法'；2. 'slow going'是固定搭配，意为'进展缓慢'；3. 'up against'意为'面临、面对'；4. 过去完成时had been表示在某个过去时间之前的状态。

sentences.en: But the president sort of shrugging that off as a waste of time and really entering this summit furious at Iran for being dishonest negotiators, in his view, because they were firing on those commercial vessels in the Strait of Hormuz.
sentences.cn: 但总统有点不以为然，认为那是浪费时间，并且真的带着对伊朗的愤怒进入这次峰会，在他看来，伊朗是不诚实的谈判者，因为他们向霍尔木兹海峡的那些商船开火。
sentences.structure: 主句（the president sort of shrugging...and...entering...），其中shrugging和entering为并列的现在分词短语作谓语，for being...为介词短语作原因状语，in his view为插入语，because引导原因状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 1. 'shrug off'意为'对...不以为然、不予理会'；2. 'sort of'是口语化程度副词；3. 'furious at...for...'结构表示'因...对...愤怒'；4. 此句为新闻中常见的描述性长句，包含多个修饰成分。

sentences.en: Remember, it was only three weeks ago that the president signed this agreement and said that, if the conflict continued, it would result in economic catastrophe.
sentences.cn: 记住，仅仅三周前总统签署了这项协议，并说如果冲突继续，将导致经济灾难。
sentences.structure: 祈使句（Remember）+ 强调句（it was...that...），强调时间状语only three weeks ago，that从句中包含and连接的两个谓语（signed和said），said后接宾语从句（that...），宾语从句中包含if引导的条件状语从句。
sentences.analysis: 1. 'Remember'是祈使句，用于提醒听众；2. 强调句结构'it was...that...'；3. 'result in'意为'导致'；4. 此句通过强调句突出时间对比。

sentences.en: You know, none of those political pressures on the president to end the war have gone away.
sentences.cn: 你知道，那些要求总统结束战争的政治压力一个都没有消失。
sentences.structure: 插入语（You know）+ 主句（none of those political pressures...have gone away），其中on the president to end the war为介词短语和不定式短语作定语修饰pressures。
sentences.analysis: 1. 'You know'是口语化插入语；2. 'none of...'作主语，谓语动词用单复数均可，此处用have为口语化；3. 'go away'意为'消失'；4. 此句体现了新闻中口语与书面语的混合。

sentences.en: We did see last night the president ordering up those retaliatory strikes on Iran.
sentences.cn: 我们昨晚确实看到总统下令对伊朗进行那些报复性打击。
sentences.structure: 主句（We did see...），其中did表示强调，ordering up...为现在分词短语作宾语补足语。
sentences.analysis: 1. 'did see'中did用于强调动词；2. 'order up'意为'下令进行'；3. 'retaliatory strikes'是新闻常用短语，意为'报复性打击'；4. 此句为强调结构。

sentences.en: And you've already seen the Iranians sort of list out the ways that they view the United States having violated this agreement.
sentences.cn: 而且你已经看到伊朗人有点列出他们认为美国违反这项协议的方式。
sentences.structure: 主句（you've already seen）+ 宾语（the Iranians sort of list out the ways）+ 定语从句（that they view the United States having violated this agreement），其中that在从句中作宾语，view...having violated为'认为...违反了'。
sentences.analysis: 1. 'sort of'是口语化程度副词；2. 'list out'意为'列出'；3. 'view...having done'结构意为'认为...做了某事'；4. 此句包含定语从句和复杂宾语结构。

sentences.en: You know, it all adds up to a furious President Trump.
sentences.cn: 你知道，这一切都导致了一个愤怒的特朗普总统。
sentences.structure: 插入语（You know）+ 主句（it all adds up to...）。
sentences.analysis: 1. 'add up to'意为'导致、意味着'；2. 'furious'是形容词，修饰President Trump；3. 此句是总结性句子，简洁有力。

sentences.en: But it was evident, listening to him this morning, that all of his grievances and all of his frustrations at NATO have not waned one bit.
sentences.cn: 但今早听他讲话，很明显，他对北约的所有不满和沮丧一点都没有减弱。
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama (it was evident...that...), dengan it sebagai subjek formal, klausa that sebagai subjek sebenarnya, listening to him this morning sebagai frase partisip present yang berfungsi sebagai keterangan.
sentences.analysis: 1. 'it is evident that...' adalah pola kalimat umum; 2. 'listening to him this morning' adalah frase partisip sebagai keterangan waktu; 3. 'wane' berarti 'melemah'; 4. 'one bit' digunakan dalam kalimat negatif, berarti 'sedikit pun (tidak)'.

sentences.en: So, this is all sort of exactly the scenario that the NATO chief, Mark Rutte, other European leaders had wanted to avoid at the summit.
sentences.cn: Jadi, ini persis seperti skenario yang ingin dihindari oleh Sekretaris Jenderal NATO Mark Rutte dan para pemimpin Eropa lainnya dalam pertemuan puncak.
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama (this is...the scenario) + klausa relatif (that the NATO chief...had wanted to avoid...), dengan Mark Rutte sebagai aposisi.
sentences.analysis: 1. 'sort of exactly' adalah ekspresi informal, berarti 'hampir persis'; 2. 'scenario' berarti 'skenario, situasi'; 3. Dalam klausa relatif, had wanted adalah past perfect, menunjukkan 'awalnya berharap'; 4. Kalimat ini mengandung aposisi dan klausa relatif.

sentences.en: But it seems pretty clear that President Trump is going to be airing all of these grievances as he's now meeting with NATO leaders behind closed doors.
sentences.cn: Tapi tampaknya cukup jelas bahwa Presiden Trump akan mengungkapkan semua keluhan ini saat bertemu secara tertutup dengan para pemimpin NATO.
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama (it seems pretty clear that...), dengan it sebagai subjek formal, klausa that sebagai subjek sebenarnya, klausa tersebut mengandung klausa keterangan waktu yang diawali as.
sentences.analysis: 1. 'it seems clear that...' adalah pola kalimat umum; 2. 'air grievances' berarti 'mengungkapkan keluhan'; 3. 'behind closed doors' berarti 'secara rahasia, di belakang pintu tertutup'; 4. Kalimat ini adalah kalimat prediktif yang umum dalam berita.

sentences.en: I think President Trump is just trying to ratchet up the visible frustration; try to put more pressure on the Iranians; see if he can get them back to a situation where they're not attacking anything.
sentences.cn: Saya pikir Presiden Trump hanya mencoba meningkatkan frustrasi yang terlihat; mencoba memberikan lebih banyak tekanan pada rakyat Iran; melihat apakah dia bisa membuat mereka kembali ke keadaan tidak menyerang apa pun.
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama (I think) + klausa objek (President Trump is just trying to...; try to...; see if...), klausa objek mengandung tiga predikat paralel (trying, try, see), dengan see diikuti klausa objek if, yang mengandung klausa relatif where yang menerangkan situation.
sentences.analysis: 1. 'ratchet up' berarti 'meningkatkan secara bertahap'; 2. 'visible frustration' berarti 'frustrasi yang terlihat'; 3. 'get sb. back to a situation where...' berarti 'membuat seseorang kembali ke keadaan...'; 4. Kalimat ini adalah kalimat panjang informal yang mengandung beberapa tindakan paralel.

sentences.en: But the problem is that we may want it or not want it, but if we are not prepared militarily, physically to defeat the regime and prevent them from doing this sort of thing, this is where this is going to go.
sentences.cn: Tapi masalahnya adalah, kita mungkin menginginkannya atau tidak, tapi jika kita tidak siap secara militer dan fisik untuk mengalahkan rezim itu dan mencegah mereka melakukan hal-hal seperti ini, inilah arah yang akan dituju.
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama (the problem is that...), klausa that sebagai klausa predikat, klausa tersebut mengandung kalimat majemuk dengan but, klausa kedua mengandung klausa kondisi if, klausa utama adalah this is where... .
sentences.analysis: 1. 'the problem is that...' adalah pola kalimat umum; 2. 'militarily, physically' adalah adverbia paralel yang menerangkan prepared; 3. 'defeat the regime and prevent them from...' adalah frase infinitif paralel; 4. 'this is where this is going to go' adalah ekspresi informal, berarti 'inilah arah yang akan dituju'.

sentences.en: He may get forced into it, but I don't think he really wants to.
sentences.cn: Dia mungkin terpaksa terlibat, tapi saya pikir dia tidak benar-benar menginginkannya.
sentences.structure: Kalimat majemuk (He may get forced into it, but I don't think...), klausa kedua mengandung klausa objek (he really wants to), dengan kata kerja yang dihilangkan.
sentences.analysis: 1. 'get forced into' berarti 'terpaksa terlibat'; 2. 'I don't think he really wants to' setelah to dihilangkan get forced into it; 3. Kalimat ini adalah ekspresi informal, singkat dan jelas.

sentences.en: So, he's trying to be tougher now to back up his negotiators, to see whether he can get things back where they were with this MOU a few weeks ago.
sentences.cn: Jadi, dia sekarang mencoba bersikap lebih tegas untuk mendukung negosiatornya, melihat apakah dia bisa mengembalikan keadaan seperti saat nota kesepahaman ditandatangani beberapa minggu lalu.
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama (he's trying to be tougher now) + keterangan tujuan (to back up...to see...), dengan see diikuti klausa objek whether, yang mengandung klausa keterangan tempat where.
sentences.analysis: 1. 'back up' berarti 'mendukung'; 2. 'get things back where they were' berarti 'mengembalikan keadaan seperti semula'; 3. 'MOU' adalah singkatan dari memorandum of understanding; 4. Kalimat ini mengandung beberapa keterangan tujuan dan klausa.

sentences.en: And I think that is what he's going to do with the Iranians here, step by step.
sentences.cn: Saya pikir itulah yang akan dia lakukan terhadap rakyat Iran di sini, langkah demi langkah.
sentences.structure: Kalimat utama (I think) + klausa objek (that is what he's going to do...), dengan what sebagai klausa predikat, step by step sebagai keterangan.
sentences.analysis: 1. 'what he's going to do' adalah klausa predikat; 2. 'step by step' berarti 'langkah demi langkah'; 3. Kalimat ini adalah kalimat penutup, singkat dan jelas.

topics.title: Hubungan AS-Iran dan Perjanjian Nuklir
topics.content: 特朗普宣布与伊朗的谅解备忘录（MOU）'结束'，指责伊朗为'败类'并称谈判是浪费时间。此前美军打击了80多个伊朗目标，并重新实施石油制裁。伊朗威胁将做出'毁灭性回应'。分析认为特朗普意在施压而非重启战争，但局势紧张。
topics.keywords: memorandum of understanding · crushing response · sanctions waivers

topics.title: 北约峰会与特朗普的抱怨
topics.content: 在土耳其安卡拉举行的北约峰会上，特朗普抱怨盟友未公平分担军费，称美国'支付比例过高'。他威胁要重新考虑对北约的承诺，但分析认为这是其惯用施压策略。北约秘书长希望避免公开冲突。

topics.title: 霍尔木兹海峡的战略重要性
topics.content: 霍尔木兹海峡（Strait of Hormuz）连接波斯湾和阿曼湾，是全球最重要的石油运输通道。伊朗试图通过控制该海峡建立'控制体系'，而美国则通过军事打击和制裁维护航行自由。近期冲突导致油价波动。
topics.keywords: Strait of Hormuz · regime of control · retaliatory strikes

topics.title: 以色列与真主党的冲突
topics.content: 美伊谅解备忘录破裂后，以色列可能获得对黎巴嫩真主党（Hezbollah）采取军事行动的'绿灯'。真主党是伊朗支持的武装组织，与以色列长期敌对。分析认为以色列将视局势变化而行动。

quiz.question: 原文中 'reimposed sanctions on Iranian oil' 中 'reimposed' 最接近的意思是？
quiz.explanation: reimpose 由前缀 re-（再次）和 impose（实施）组成，意为'重新实施'。文中指美国在取消制裁后再次对伊朗石油实施制裁。

quiz.question: 特朗普称伊朗为 'scum'，这个词在语境中的含义是？
quiz.explanation: scum 原意为'浮渣'，引申为'败类、人渣'，是强烈的贬义词。特朗普用此词表达对伊朗的极度不满。

quiz.question: 短语 'ratchet up' 在 'ratchet up the visible frustration' 中的意思是？
quiz.explanation: ratchet up 意为'逐步增加'，像棘轮一样一步步上升。文中指特朗普逐步加剧公开的挫败感。

quiz.question: 根据报道，特朗普宣布与伊朗的谅解备忘录'结束'的直接原因是什么？
options: B. 伊朗攻击霍尔木兹海峡附近的船只
quiz.explanation: 报道明确指出，特朗普的言论是在美军打击伊朗目标并重新实施制裁之后，而制裁是为了报复伊朗对霍尔木兹海峡附近船只的袭击。

quiz.question: 前大使 Kurt Volker 认为特朗普对伊朗的强硬表态主要目的是什么？
options: B. 施压伊朗回到谈判桌
quiz.explanation: Volker 表示特朗普是在'逐步加剧可见的挫败感'，试图'给伊朗施加更多压力'，并'看看能否让他们回到不攻击任何东西的状态'，即施压而非重启战争。

quiz.question: 关于以色列与真主党的冲突，报道暗示了什么？
options: A. 以色列将立即停火
B. 以色列可能获得攻击真主党的自由
C. 真主党将攻击以色列
D. 美国将阻止以色列行动
quiz.explanation: 报道中 Volker 说'以色列肯定会这样理解'，即如果停火结束，以色列会认为有了'绿灯'，可以自由地再次攻击真主党。
